channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Reverse-engineering an MMO Aion 2's network protocol to build a real-time DPS meter (Rust + Tauri)
item.description: Disclosure: this is a write-up about my own side project — a combat analytics tool for AION 2. No affiliation with the game's publisher. Links at the end. Architecture A Windows desktop app: Rust...

item.title: When Code "Works" by Accident: Hunting Down an Undocumented Fallback in node-pg-migrate 🔍
item.description: This is a submission for DEV's Summer Bug Smash: Smash Stories powered by Sentry. We've all spent hours debugging a broken piece of code. But what happens when your code is supposed to fail, but...

item.title: Running an LLM agent entirely in your browser
item.description: TL;DR: I fine-tuned LiquidAI's LFM2.5 (230M and 350M) into a generic front-end agent that runs entirely in the browser - no server, no API key, no cloud costs. It doesn't just chat; it calls real...

item.title: What Is Git-Aware Project Management?
item.description: Software teams often plan work in one place and build software somewhere else. Tasks live on a project board. But the real work happens in the IDE, Git branches, commits, pushes, pull requests,...

item.title: Are computers truly random? How chaos powers randomness.
item.description: You would have come across randomness on a computer at a particular point; whether it is playing a game where assets spawn at random locations, writing code that requires random numbers, shuffling...

item.title: Lattice: The Pure Rust LLM Engine Optimized for Apple Silicon
item.description: Native Local AI on Mac: Meet Lattice Running Large Language Models (LLMs) locally has become standard practice for developers seeking privacy and speed. However, configuring runtimes usually means...

item.title: Building a Complex WordPress Platform from Scratch
item.description: Hi my name is Perceval Hasselman and I'm and Building a simple WordPress website is one thing. Building a scalable platform with user accounts, custom dashboards, search functionality, premium...

item.title: Have You Outgrown Prompt Engineering?
item.description: For a while, prompt engineering felt like a search for the perfect phrase. Give the model a role. Add a few examples. Request a structured format. Refine the wording until the response improves....

item.title: My Demo Script Found a Production Bug on Its First Run: A Tiny Post-Mortem
item.description: I built a demo script to show off a project. It failed on step 2 — and in doing so, it found a real bug that had been sitting in the codebase the whole time, invisible to a fully green test suite....

item.title: Build One AI Tool Server, Call It From Three Different Agents (MCP Explained)
item.description: Have you ever wanted to give an AI assistant a new ability — like generating images — and have that ability work in any AI tool you use, not just one? That's exactly what this project does, and...

item.title: Roblox is shutting down its video chat service
item.description: Roblox will be shutting down Roblox Connect, its video calling service introduced in 2023. Roblox Connect let you video chat with other people using your Roblox avatar, which would be able to...

item.title: AI slop movies are the new direct-to-video cash grabs
item.description: This weekend, cinephiles across the world will march to their local theaters to feast their eyes on Christopher Nolan's new adaptation of The Odyssey. It's on track to rake in anywhere between...

item.title: Windows 0-day drops the same day Microsoft releases record number of patches
item.description: HiveLegacy is a "powerful primitive" that's likely capable of other nefarious actions.

item.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
item.description: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

item.title: TuxBot v3 Evolution Shows Signs of LLM-Assisted IoT Botnet Development
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a previously unreported Internet-of-Things (IoT) botnet framework dubbed TuxBot v3 Evolution that shows signs of being developed with assistance...

item.title: Enshittification
item.description: Bryan, a food broker from Michigan, wasn't sure if he'd be able to make it to urgent care in time. He started feeling off on Thursday, and by Saturday, he was having to use the bathroom every 15...

item.title: AI Isn’t Smarter Than a Baby—Yet
item.description: Babies are tremendous learning machines, and key advances for AI may soon be found in the architecture of their little brains.

item.title: Something’s glowing on the Pixel 11’s camera bar
item.description: A new teaser for Google's upcoming Pixel 11 lineup reveals that the phones will feature some kind of glowing orb on the camera bar, as reported by 9to5Google. Google's store page for the Pixel 11...

item.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
item.description: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.

item.title: Thinking Machines Lab Drops Its First Model
item.description: Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter open source model, was trained to understand video and audio. It could help Thinking Machines establish itself among competitors like Anthropic and OpenAI.

item.title: Thinking Machines amps up its bet against one-size-fits-all AI with its first open model, Inkling
item.description: It's the company's first public proof point after a year and a half spent building AI infrastructure largely out of public view.

item.title: Suno snatched millions of songs from YouTube, Genius, and Deezer
item.description: Suno data obtained in a hacking incident has exposed that the AI music generator was trained by scraping millions of songs and lyrics from online audio platforms, including YouTube Music, Deezer,...

item.title: What building Shippy taught us about building agents
Model Routing Is Simple. Until It Isn’t.
Lucid’s bankruptcy rumor is a bad sign for the EV future
item.description: Lucid Motors found itself in a tough bind this week, fending off bankruptcy rumors and watching its stock price plunge as a result. The company quickly denied the report, calling it "completely...

item.title: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
item.description: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest...

item.title: Hack suggests AI music generator Suno scraped YouTube for training data
item.description: The hacker used an employee's credentials to access source code, which revealed how Suno scraped decades of audio.

item.title: Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations
item.description: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search. The deal will bolster Whatnot’s personalization and...

item.title: Netflix tries to recapture Stranger Things magic with nostalgic re-release
item.description: Netflix just can't let Stranger Things go. Since the final episode aired on New Year's Eve there's been a behind-the-scenes documentary, an animated series that expands the story, and now, on the...

item.title: Samsung shows off ‘brand new shape’ for Z Fold 8 in Spider-Man teaser
item.description: Samsung gave a sneak peek of the "brand new shape" for its upcoming Galaxy Z Fold 8 in a new teaser for Spider-Man: Brand New Day. The video includes a few shots of Spidey taking a foldable phone...
